introduction: A complication of rheumatic fever and common cause of cardiac failure in Nigeria. In Africans it manifests later compared to Caucasians. The mitral valve is most affected, followed by the aortic, then the tricuspid. The lesions can occur in various combinations of regurgitation and stenosis.

features: Cough with production of frothy sputum
Small volume pulse, which may be irregular

drug: Treat for heart failure if present
Benzathine penicillin 720 mg (1.2 million units) intramuscularly monthly for life
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Penicillin may cause hypersensitivity reaction / anaphylaxis. Caution in patients with a history of penicillin allergy

complications: Arrhythmias e.g. atrial fibrillation, heart block
prevention: Personal hygiene and good sanitation to prevent recurrence of rheumatic fever
